title: CSS Basics
description: Learn CSS to style your HTML pages with colors, fonts, and layouts

blocks.content: What is CSS?

CSS stands for **Cascading Style Sheets**. It's used to style and layout web pages — for example, to alter the font, color, size, and spacing of your content.

While HTML provides structure, CSS provides **presentation**:
- Colors and backgrounds
- Fonts and text styling
- Sizing and spacing
- Layout and positioning
CSS Syntax

CSS uses **rules** to style elements. A CSS rule has two parts:
- **Selector** - Which HTML element(s) to style
- **Declaration** - What styling to apply
And here's what those styles do to some HTML — a live preview:

blocks.html:
This is a Heading

This paragraph is blue, 16px text.

Every paragraph uses these styles automatically.
blocks.caption: Live preview: CSS rules applied to HTML elements

blocks.content:
Breaking it down:
- **Selector** (`p`) - Select all paragraph elements
- **Property** (`color`) - What aspect to style
- **Value** (`blue`) - The styling value
- The `{}` contains all declarations for that selector
- Each declaration ends with `;`
Adding CSS to HTML
There are three ways to add CSS to your HTML. We recommend the **external stylesheet** method.
Method 1: External Stylesheet (Recommended)

Create a separate `.css` file and link it in your HTML:

**HTML File:**

Welcome

This page has styling!
**CSS File (styles.css):**
Method 2: Inline Styles
Add style directly to HTML elements (not recommended for large projects):

Welcome

This paragraph is red.
Method 3: Style Tag in Head

Welcome

Hello world!
Live preview of the code above:

blocks.html:
Welcome

Hello world!
blocks.caption: Live preview: inline <style> tag styling the page

blocks.content: Common CSS Properties

**Colors:**
- `color` - Text color
- `background-color` - Background color

**Fonts:**
- `font-family` - The typeface (Arial, Georgia, etc.)
- `font-size` - Size of text
- `font-weight` - Boldness (normal, bold, 700, etc.)

**Text:**
- `text-align` - Alignment (left, center, right)
- `text-decoration` - Underline, overline, etc.
- `line-height` - Space between lines

**Spacing:**
- `margin` - Space outside an element
- `padding` - Space inside an element
- `border` - Line around an element

**Size:**
- `width` - Element width
- `height` - Element height
Here's a live demo that shows many of these properties in action:

blocks.html:
CSS Properties Demo

font-weight: bold

font-style: italic

font-size: 24px

font-size: 11px

color: red

color: darkgreen
